original_hypothesis.title: Interest index uint128 overflow can permanently brick asset operations under extreme rate model outputs
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: SupplyIndex and BorrowIndex are stored as uint128 and updated via SafeCast.toUint128(). If indices exceed uint128 max, updates revert, potentially bricking all interactions that call updateInterestIndexs for that asset (deposits, withdrawals, borrows, repays). While extreme, this can happen if an interest rate model is misconfigured or returns unboundedly high rates for long periods.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Phase A invariant INV-MONO-001 flagged irreversible DoS from overflow. This is operationally important: rate model upgrades/config mistakes can create a hard failure state that is difficult to recover from without migrations.
deployment_assumptions: Admin configures/updates a rate model that can output extremely high borrowRate/supplyRate, or utilization/rate spikes can drive outputs high
Asset experiences long periods without interactions so exp grows large, or rate itself is high enough over shorter time
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: Rate models are externally set via configuration (e.g., ConfigureLogic.executeAddAssetGroup / executeSetAssetLendingRate / executeSetAssetYieldRate). No explicit bounds enforcement on returned rates is shown in-scope.
preconditions: Rate model returns rates high enough that calculateLinearInterest/calculateCompoundedInterest causes next index > type(uint128).max
Any user/admin action triggers updateInterestIndexs for that asset afterward

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: No guardrails ensuring rate model outputs keep indices within uint128 bounds
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: Yes (rate model configuration), or exploit if IRM itself compromised

operational_questions: What are the maximum possible rates produced by each deployed IRM under worst-case utilization?
What is the longest plausible time delta between updates on the target chain(s)?
Are there emergency procedures to freeze/disable an asset before overflow occurs?
validation_requirements: Compute index growth bounds and time-to-overflow under worst-case rates
Fuzz/simulate with extreme rate values to confirm revert behavior and blast radius
Review operational playbooks for IRM changes and monitoring thresholds

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Induce or wait for IRM misconfiguration/extreme rate regime
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Allow time for index growth beyond uint128
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Trigger any operation that calls updateInterestIndexs to brick the asset path

potential_operational_impact.administrative_compromise: Not required if misconfiguration is accidental; attacker can benefit by targeting chaos
potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Permanent DoS for asset operations; user funds potentially stuck until migration
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: All interactions touching the bricked asset

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Rapidly increasing borrowIndex/supplyIndex approaching uint128 limit
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Interest update reverts for a specific asset
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: IRM changes followed by abnormal index growth

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
